aliases: Matcha Latte with Mochi and Jelly

english: Matcha Latte with Mochi and Jelly

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for matcha latte with mochi and jelly toppings. Closest parent 'tea-based beverage' (FOODON_03301099) used as anchor.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code AN0AN (Tea and tea-based beverages) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for matcha latte with milk and confectionery toppings.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 74964007 'Beverage (substance)' used as broadest defensible anchor. No specific SNOMED code for matcha latte with mochi and jelly.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately one 500 ml cup containing the matcha latte base with matcha cloud foam, matcha mochi, and matcha jelly toppings, as served by HEYTEA at Marina Bay Sands, Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Matcha latte with whole milk — nutrient composition estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data (matcha powder, whole milk, sugar, mochi, jelly) and peer-reviewed matcha beverage literature
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central; supplemented by published matcha beverage composition data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB Singapore entry exists for HEYTEA Triple Supreme Matcha Latte. Nutrient values are estimated by component: whole milk (USDA FDC #01077), matcha powder (USDA FDC #2003571), cane sugar (USDA FDC #19335), mochi (rice-based confection, USDA FDC #168880 as proxy), and matcha jelly (konjac/agar-based, estimated as negligible macro contribution). Proportions estimated from typical HEYTEA-style matcha latte formulation: ~60% milk, ~5% matcha powder, ~10% sugar syrup, ~15% mochi, ~10% jelly. Values expressed per 100 g of the combined beverage-with-toppings.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — milk-based sweetened beverages and matcha tea analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for HEYTEA Triple Supreme Matcha Latte. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on analogous milk-based sweetened matcha beverage with added sugar and rice-based mochi. Whole milk GI ~27 (University of Sydney), sucrose GI ~65, mochi (glutinous rice) GI ~86. Weighted blend of components with milk as dominant volume fraction yields estimated composite GI of approximately 55. Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (55 × 71.0 g available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 39.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Moderate sodium content (~190 mg per 500 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: A single 500 ml serving contributes approximately 190 mg sodium, which is within acceptable limits for most hypertensive individuals, but added sugar and caloric load should be monitored if consumed daily.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~39 per serving) driven by cane sugar, mochi (glutinous rice), and sweetened jelly
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit this beverage due to its high glycemic load of approximately 39 per serving; the combination of free sugars from cane sugar syrup and high-GI glutinous rice mochi can cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation, and the 'no sugar added' option should be selected where available.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from whole milk and moderate added sugar content
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The saturated fat contribution (~7.5 g per 500 g serving) from whole milk and the high free-sugar content (~52.5 g per serving) are relevant for cardiovascular risk management; individuals with cardiovascular disease should choose the no-sugar or reduced-sugar option and limit frequency of consumption.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Triple Supreme Matcha Latte is a premium layered matcha beverage sold by HEYTEA, a Chinese tea brand with outlets in Singapore including Marina Bay Sands. The drink is constructed from multiple matcha-forward components: a matcha cloud foam layer (aerated matcha cream), a base matcha tea or matcha milk layer, chewy matcha mochi pieces (glutinous rice cakes flavoured with matcha), and matcha jelly (likely konjac or agar-based). The entire drink is made with real whole milk and sweetened with cane sugar by default, though a no-sugar or reduced-sugar option is available. The beverage contains dairy and soy allergens. Texturally, it offers a contrast between the silky foam, the liquid matcha milk base, the chewy mochi, and the soft jelly. Flavour is dominated by the grassy, umami bitterness of matcha balanced by the sweetness of sugar and the creaminess of milk. Nutritionally, per 100 g the drink provides approximately 89 kcal, 2.8 g protein, 2.5 g fat, and 14.2 g carbohydrate with 10.5 g sugars. A full 500 ml serving delivers roughly 445 kcal and a glycemic load of approximately 39, making it a high-glycemic-load indulgence. Clinically, it is not suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes without significant sugar reduction. The matcha component provides L-theanine and catechin antioxidants, which have been associated with modest cardiovascular and metabolic benefits in peer-reviewed literature, but these benefits are substantially offset by the high sugar and caloric content of the full-sugar version. Priced at SGD 9.90, it is positioned as a premium dessert beverage in the Singapore market.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or any approved primary source for HEYTEA Triple Supreme Matcha Latte as a composite product. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FDC component data for whole milk, matcha powder, cane sugar, glutinous rice mochi, and agar/konjac jelly using typical HEYTEA-style formulation proportions. GI is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for component foods. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated from USDA FoodData Central entries for whole milk (FDC #01077), matcha powder (FDC #2003571), cane sugar (FDC #19335), and mochi proxy (FDC #168880). No single approved-source entry covers this composite product. 2. vitamin_d_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported across the component sources used for this estimation. vitamin_e_mg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources at sufficient precision for a composite estimate. selenium_mcg is set to -1 as it is not reported in the component sources. 3. trans_fat_g is 0.0; confirmed as negligible/trace in whole milk and matcha powder per USDA FDC data. 4. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this product exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load per 500 g serving calculated as (55 × 71.0 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 39.1. 5. Halal status is not confirmed; HEYTEA Singapore halal certification status was not verifiable from approved sources at time of processing. 6. The description notes that sugar-free sweetener may be out of stock; nutrient values reflect the default full-sugar cane sugar formulation. 7. Serving size of 500 g is an estimate for a standard large cup; actual weight may vary by outlet and customisation.
